Off Calendar Apr-25-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, Compliance With Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT PANZER REAL ESTATE's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, Requests For Production Of Documents, Compliance With Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. This is a wrongful eviction action, together with related causes of action for various breaches of warranties and covenants governing the rental and habitability of residential units and claims for damages caused by tortious conduct by defendants. Plaintiff was a tenant of a property owned and/or managed by one or more of the defendants, including defendant Panzer Real Estate. After defendant challenges to the sufficiency of plaintiff's complaint resulted in the filing of a Second Amended Complaint, defendant Panzer Real Estate propounded written discovery to plaintiff. Extensions of time to serve answers and to file a motion to compel were granted on a number of occasions. Eventually the subject motion to compel was filed by Panzer Real Estate directed to plaintiff's allegedly insufficient and untimely responses to form and special interrogatories and failure to produce any documents in response to said defendant's Request for Production of Documents. There has been no opposition to the motion to compel filed by plaintiff. Accordingly, for good cause shown, the motion to compel further responses to form and special interrogatories, and the production of documents responsive to the Request for Production of Documents, is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall provide responses, and produce documents, no later than 15 days after the filing date of the order granting the instant motion. The moving party shall prepare a proposed order in accordance with this ruling and, in doing so, comply with Ruled 3.1312 of the Rules of Court. The proposed order shall be forwarded to psw@hassard.com for signature.

On the subject of sanctions, counsel for the moving party is entitled to sanctions pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.030(a) as against plaintiff and her counsel or record. The amount thereof requested in the moving papers is, however, excessive given the nature and complexity, or lack thereof, of this case. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $4,800.00.

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
